understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', with all goals occupied at the same time. Completing the final level ends the run in a WIN state.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece can be repositioned by CLICKing: if the clicked square is a valid reachable destination (apparently requiring a clear, unobstructed line/valid path under the level's movement rules), the currently selected piece moves there; otherwise it stays put and the step is still consumed. Obstacles/walls block movement and make certain clicks have no effect. The attempted clicks that repeatedly failed suggest destinations are not freely reachable—movement is constrained (likely by line-of-sight/slide rules) and blocked by intervening terrain or occupied/invalid tiles. Steps are limited; once steps reach 0 the run ends even if goals are not satisfied.

orient_history: Arrow MOVE actions attempt to shift the selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but collisions with walls can fully block movement. CLICK likely changes state by selecting/placing a piece at a chosen location (possibly onto the goal) since movement is otherwise impossible.

Hypothesis: CLICK changes the selected piece when clicking on another selectable; MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3px) if not blocked by walls/other pieces. Need to place both selectables onto the goal area simultaneously to win.

CLICK likely can place/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked grid-aligned empty/goal cell; clicking non-grid-aligned pixels (like [35,17]) may fail due to collision/alignment. Arrow moves are standard 1-cell moves but are impossible here due to walls.

CLICK likely selects a selectable under the cursor (does not move pieces). MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) and is blocked by collidable walls. Since current piece is immobile, we should switch selection to the other piece and reassess its possible moves.

Working hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected (engine reports it as the 'selected piece moved' to that selectable's position). MOVE_* then attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels), but the current piece is blocked in all directions. Next I should select the other piece and see if it has any unblocked movement or can interact differently with the goal.

CLICKing on a selectable switches which piece is selected (the 'player' position jumps to that piece). Movement of the selected piece is done via MOVE_* actions, but may be possible only for the other selectable if it has an open adjacent cell.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection; MOVE attempts to move selected by 1 cell (3px) but both pieces may have different interactions (e.g., one might move/affect walls). Next step is to select the other piece and reassess its movement options.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate only if the destination is reachable under a rule (likely same row/column / unobstructed); otherwise nothing happens. Arrow MOVE_* shows immediate adjacency collisions but may not be the main movement method. Need to test a same-column CLICK to see if vertical movement is allowed.

Hypothesis: CLICK may only act when targeting a valid destination tile/object (possibly including goal) but coordinate/anchor may matter (e.g., must click inside goal area near its center). Otherwise, only swapping with the other selectable works. Need to test whether goal can accept a CLICK-placement.

CLICK likely issues a move-to command if you click an empty/goal cell; earlier CLICKs on the other selectable mainly swapped selection. Previous failed goal clicks may have missed the actual goal cell anchor (needs cell-aligned pixel, multiples of 3).

Clicks seem to only change which selectable is selected (not move to arbitrary empty/goal tiles). To make progress we likely must select the other piece and see if it has any free adjacent space for MOVE actions.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a piece when clicking on a selectable; clicking on a goal tile may place/teleport the currently selected piece onto the goal (since normal movement is fully blocked). Need to get all goal tiles/goal region occupied simultaneously by available selectables.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected/active; movement availability depends on the selected piece’s local surroundings. Need to find which piece (if any) can navigate or interact to reach/cover the goal region.

CLICK likely changes interaction state (either toggling which piece is selected, or picking up/placing a piece). Since movement is fully blocked by walls, winning probably requires a click-based pick-and-place or teleport mechanic rather than MOVE_* navigation.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves 1 cell (3px) but requires empty space; CLICK can interact with tiles/objects and may remove/alter walls (or otherwise change traversability). Since movement is fully blocked, we likely must CLICK a wall tile to open a path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move/teleport the selected piece to the clicked pixel position if it is a valid free tile (including goal). Prior goal clicks may have failed due to not clicking a valid exact cell anchor within the goal’s 3x3 cell area; movement keys are unusable due to walls.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (the log reports this as the 'selected piece moved'). Different selectables may have different available moves depending on their local surroundings, so we should switch to the other piece to check if it has an exit path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches control to that piece; MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked by walls. To make progress, we must select the other piece and see if it has any available movement routes toward the goal area.

CLICK likely changes the selected piece when clicking another selectable (logs report it as the selected piece 'moving'). Direct MOVE_* is impossible while selected piece is boxed in by walls. Next step is to switch selection and test interactions toward the goal.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled (the 'player') when clicking on another selectable; MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels) but collisions with walls can fully block movement. We may need to control the other selectable to find any available movement path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK primarily interacts with selectables (possibly changing which piece is selected or swapping positions), while MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece one cell when not blocked. Since the currently selected piece is fully boxed-in, we should try selecting the other selectable to see if it has any legal moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: direct MOVE_* is impossible from current pocket; CLICKing on the other selectable at [18,21] likely switches which piece is active (and/or swaps their positions), potentially giving a different movement adjacency that can progress toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* is grid-step movement but both pieces are enclosed; CLICK may relocate the selected piece to a clicked valid tile/object (possibly requiring clicking the goal’s anchor position rather than an interior pixel). Previous CLICKs inside the goal region ([36,18]) had no effect, so I will try clicking the goal’s exact top-left coordinate from the object list.

MOVE_* only moves one cell and is blocked by adjacent walls; CLICK may be the intended mechanic to switch which selectable is active and/or teleport between selectable positions, which might be necessary to access the enclosed goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection; MOVE_* attempts to slide the selected piece but both pieces are currently enclosed by walls. There may still be a precise goal-cell pixel to CLICK that triggers a teleport/placement, so I will probe an exact cell-aligned coordinate inside the goal cluster.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected; then MOVE_* moves the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked. Since the current piece is fully blocked, we should switch selection to the other piece and re-check its movement options; it may have an open route to the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection to that piece (the 'selected piece moved' log reflects selection changing). MOVE_* attempts move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) but are blocked by adjacent walls depending on location.

Hypothesis: CLICK may move/teleport the currently selected selectable to the clicked pixel coordinate if it corresponds to a valid empty/goal tile; earlier failures may be due to clicking non-interactive coordinates. Need to find a CLICK coordinate within the goal region that is accepted.

Hypothesis: Arrow MOVE_* only works if adjacent cell is not a wall; currently blocked. CLICK may have additional interactions besides swapping/teleporting between selectables—possibly toggling/removing a wall if clicked. We need to create mobility toward the goal region quickly.

Clicks seem to be the only way to reposition a selectable (arrow movement is fully blocked). Prior goal clicks may have missed an allowed goal-aligned landing pixel; trying a goal cell-aligned pixel (multiple of 3) inside the + region may move the selected piece onto the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection; CLICK on empty/goal attempts to move the currently selected piece to that pixel location, but may require hitting a valid/registered point (e.g., center of goal tile) and/or fails if considered blocked. With no movement possible via arrows, the only plausible winning attempt with 1 step is a precise CLICK into the goal area (try center rather than top-left).

Moves/CLICKs consume steps; with 0 steps remaining, no further meaningful interaction is possible this level. The selected piece is enclosed by walls so even with steps it could not move via cardinal MOVE actions; previous successful CLICKs appeared to swap/select between two fixed selectable positions, but the goal is unreachable within remaining budget.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', with all goals occupied at the same time. Completing the final level ends the run in a WIN state.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece can be repositioned by CLICKing: if the clicked square is a valid reachable destination (apparently requiring a clear, unobstructed line/valid path under the level's movement rules), the currently selected piece moves there; otherwise it stays put and the step is still consumed. Obstacles/walls block movement and make certain clicks have no effect. The attempted clicks that repeatedly failed suggest destinations are not freely reachable—movement is constrained (likely by line-of-sight/slide rules) and blocked by intervening terrain or occupied/invalid tiles. Steps are limited; once steps reach 0 the run ends even if goals are not satisfied.
